response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced generalist with strong testing culture and open-source leadership, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 versus the required 8 years, and his large-scale infrastructure exposure (about two years of high-throughput DLang microservices at Dunnhumby, plus Kubernetes deployment of a side project) falls well short of the 5-year requirement. His recent work is web and product engineering in TypeScript/Node.js, not cloud compute platform architecture, and he has no formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers.
strengths: Genuine low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers, caching)
Strong software craftsmanship and testing background (TDD, CI/CD, authored test frameworks, ran multi-day courses and workshops)
Demonstrated mentorship and knowledge-sharing plus long-running ownership of an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ minimum — only ~7 months of professional C/C++ work, and none since 2015
Large-scale infrastructure/distributed-systems experience totals roughly 2 years, short of the 5-year requirement
No production cloud-architecture or formal technical-leadership track record; recent roles are full-stack product work, not compute platform strategy
